prediction.summary: A sustained brute force attack was detected targeting multiple user accounts via SSH. An external IP address, 203.0.113.45, made numerous failed password attempts against at least 11 different usernames over a short period before the connection was closed. This activity indicates an adversary attempting to gain unauthorized access to the system.

recommendations: Immediately block the source IP address 203.0.113.45 at the firewall or host level to prevent further attempts.
Review SSH logs for any successful logins from 203.0.113.45 or any of the targeted usernames around the time of the brute force attempt.
Verify that account lockout policies are properly configured and enforced for SSH to mitigate brute force attacks.
Consider implementing stronger authentication mechanisms for SSH, such as key-based authentication, multi-factor authentication (MFA), or IP-based access restrictions.
Review the list of targeted usernames to ensure they are legitimate accounts and not default or easily guessable usernames.
